text: Welcome everyone to Meridian's all-hands kickoff for Q1 2024. We're launching three major projects this quarter: Atlas (our new transaction ledger), Phoenix (event streaming), and Horizon (the customer dashboard). Alice leads Atlas and Phoenix, Carol leads Horizon.

Exciting news: Meridian has closed our Series A. This gives us runway to scale Atlas and Phoenix, and to hire the engineers we need for Orion. Dave is starting on Orion next month. Thank you to everyone who contributed to the fundraise.

Reminder: All Q1 architectural decisions need to be documented in Confluence. Alice has set up the Engineering space. Use the ADR template for any decision involving the core stack — Postgres, Kafka, Kubernetes, or TypeScript.

The Atlas v0.1 is live in staging. Postgres backend, FastAPI layer, Redis cache. Alice and Grace are doing the final load testing this week. The target is 10k transactions per second before we go to production.

Phoenix Kafka migration is done. Bob's team completed the full switch from RabbitMQ to Kafka ahead of schedule. The event streaming is now running at 600k messages per second. Big win for the infrastructure team.

Meridian company values reminder: we build for reliability first. Every architectural decision — from choosing Postgres over MongoDB, to Kafka over RabbitMQ — is made with long-term reliability in mind. Not just what's trendy.

New hire: Grace is joining as senior backend engineer this Monday, working with Alice on Atlas. She's a Postgres expert who has scaled ledger systems to millions of transactions per day. Great addition to the team.

Q1 milestone: Horizon Beta is live for 50 internal users. Carol's team delivered TypeScript + React dashboard on schedule. The GraphQL API is responding under 100ms. Excellent work from the frontend team.

All hands recording is in Google Drive — Meridian Q1 All Hands deck. Alice covered the Atlas architecture, Bob covered Phoenix, Carol demoed Horizon, Dave showed the Orion ML prototype. Find it in the Leadership channel or Drive.
Hiring update: we're looking for a senior ML engineer to support Dave on Orion. The role requires PyTorch experience and familiarity with Kubernetes. Reach out to Quinn in HR if you have referrals.

Nathan from sales just closed Meridian's first enterprise customer! They'll be using Atlas for their transaction processing. Alice is joining the onboarding call to walk them through the Postgres architecture. Big deal for the company.

Hiro is joining the Titan infrastructure team under Frank this week. He's bringing deep Kubernetes and Docker experience. First priority: improving the CI/CD pipeline build times and the Terraform deployment workflow.
Orion is now in closed alpha. Dave has the PyTorch model running inference in under 50ms on GCP. The FastAPI endpoint is ready. Horizon will integrate Orion predictions into the dashboard in Q2.

The Meridian data platform is coming together: Atlas (Postgres) → Phoenix (Kafka) → Orion (PyTorch) → Horizon (React/TypeScript). Each layer is solid. Thanks to Alice, Bob, Dave, Carol, and Frank for executing this vision.

Morgan from product is now leading the Prism initiative — real-time analytics on top of Atlas and Phoenix. Target Q2 launch. Morgan will work closely with Bob on the Kafka Streams design and Alice on the Postgres read model.

Company-wide reminder: all sensitive data stays in Postgres. No production data in Notion or Slack. The Jira tickets for Atlas should not contain customer transaction data. Frank has updated the security policy in Confluence.

Rachel from legal has reviewed the data residency requirements for our EU customers. Atlas Postgres data must stay in the EU region. Frank is setting up a dedicated GCP europe-west1 cluster for EU data. TBD on timeline.

Meridian Q1 recap post is live on the company Notion. Covers: Atlas launch (Postgres), Phoenix Kafka migration, Horizon Beta (TypeScript/React), Orion Alpha (PyTorch/GCP). Strong quarter — we delivered on every major milestone.
Tyler from design just joined to support Carol on Horizon. Tyler brings experience with design systems. First project is the Horizon component library in TypeScript with Storybook documentation.

The v2 roadmap planning session is scheduled for next week. Eve will lead. Key questions to decide: do we launch Prism in Q2 or Q3? How do we scale Atlas to 100k transactions per second? What's the Orion ML strategy for customers?

Meridian has a new Jira board for tracking all Atlas, Phoenix, Orion, Horizon, and Titan work. Bob set up the sprint structure. Alice is the overall project lead. All engineering work should be tracked in Jira from now on.

Sam from data science is joining to support Dave on Orion. Sam has published research on transformer models and has deep PyTorch expertise. They'll be running experiments on GCP and contributing to the ML pipeline in Airflow.

Important: the Meridian platform monorepo is on GitHub. All code for Atlas, Phoenix, Orion, Horizon, and Titan is now in one place. Alice and Frank set up the branch protection rules and the CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions.

Observability update from Frank: Prometheus + Grafana dashboards are live for all services. Sentry is catching errors in Atlas and Horizon. Datadog APM is running on Phoenix. We have full visibility across the Meridian platform now.

The Horizon public API docs are live. Carol wrote the GraphQL schema documentation. REST endpoints for backward compatibility are also documented. The docs site is built with React and TypeScript, deployed on GCP via Frank's Kubernetes setup.

We are opening access to the Horizon beta to 500 customers next week. Alice reviewed the Atlas load capacity — we're comfortable at 5x current load. Frank confirmed the Kubernetes autoscaling is configured. Bob verified Kafka consumer health.

Meridian's security audit is next month. Rachel is coordinating with external auditors. Focus areas: Atlas Postgres encryption at rest, JWT token handling across services, OAuth flow security in Horizon. Frank owns the remediation plan.

The decision on the v2 pricing model is pending Eve and Nathan's analysis. Open question: usage-based vs seat-based? Should Orion ML inference calls be metered separately from Atlas transactions? TBD — decision needed by end of Q2.

Blake from product has been added to the Atlas project. Blake will help Alice prioritize the Q2 roadmap — focusing on what enterprise customers need in the transaction ledger. First meeting is Monday with Alice, Eve, and Nathan.

The Apex service — customer-facing analytics export — is in the early design phase. Morgan is leading the design. Should it use the Atlas Postgres read replica or a separate data warehouse? Open question: Postgres vs BigQuery for analytics.

James from support has a new escalation process for Atlas issues. Any Postgres replication lag or Atlas transaction failures go directly to Alice and Frank via PagerDuty. Horizon API errors go to Carol. Phoenix Kafka issues go to Bob.

Uma from HR is hosting the Q2 planning offsite next month. All team leads are invited: Alice (Atlas), Bob (Phoenix), Carol (Horizon), Dave (Orion), Frank (Titan), Morgan (Prism), Eve (Product). Agenda will be shared in Notion.

Avery has joined as the new technical writer. First project: documenting the Atlas transaction ledger API for enterprise customers. Working with Alice and Carol to capture the Postgres data model and the GraphQL/REST API design.

The Meridian investor update is out. Key metrics: Atlas handling 12k transactions per second, Phoenix processing 600k events per second, Horizon beta at 92% satisfaction. Orion ML predictions are 94% accurate on the test dataset.

Kyle from finance has a question for Alice: how should we account for Postgres infrastructure costs in the Atlas unit economics model? Frank has the GCP billing breakdown by service. Kyle, Alice, and Frank will review it this week.

The new onboarding doc for engineering is in Notion. Covers: setting up the monorepo, Docker environment, running Atlas and Phoenix locally, connecting to the dev Postgres instance and the Kafka cluster. Dave and Frank wrote it.

Wendy from partnerships has signed three new connector integrations with enterprise data providers. These will feed into Atlas via the Phoenix Kafka pipeline. Alice and Bob are reviewing the API contracts this week.

Security reminder from Rachel: all GitHub secrets for GCP, Postgres, Kafka, and JWT must be rotated quarterly. Frank owns the rotation schedule. Last rotation was March 1. Next rotation due June 1. Critical for Titan and Atlas.

Skyler from design is prototyping the v2 Horizon redesign in Figma. Working closely with Carol on the TypeScript component architecture. The new design system will be documented in Storybook. Target: Q2 design review with Eve.

The Meridian developer portal is launching next month. It will show the Atlas API docs, Orion ML API reference, and the Horizon webhook integration guide. Carol is leading the React frontend, Avery is writing the docs content.

Victor from legal has reviewed the open source licensing for all Meridian dependencies. Postgres (PostgreSQL License), Kafka (Apache 2.0), React (MIT), FastAPI (MIT), PyTorch (BSD). All compatible with our commercial license.
